name: United Roosevelt Savings Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and business banking with early direct deposit and local merchant discounts.
description_long:
United Roosevelt Savings Bank (URSB) is a community-focused financial institution based in Carteret, New Jersey, with NMLS #786209 and routing #221270240. The bank emphasizes FDIC insurance protection backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, positioning itself as a safe deposit institution for personal and business customers.

URSB offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), retirement accounts, personal lending, mortgage loans with rate calculators, business checking and lending, credit cards, and merchant services. The bank partners with TIB for payment processing solutions and offers the BaZing local discount network providing deals with retailers in the Carteret area. Notable features include early direct deposit access (up to 2 days early, up to $10,000 per deposit with no enrollment required), a mobile app, online banking platforms for both personal and business customers, AllPoint ATM access, remote deposit capabilities, and safe deposit boxes.

URSB distinguishes itself through community engagement, including sponsorship of the Carteret Performing Arts Center and participation in Financial Literacy Awareness Month initiatives. The bank maintains a 5-star rating from Bauer-Financial and emphasizes fraud protection education through partnership with the American Bankers Association. Their merchant services division offers transparent fee structures, 24/7 customer support, and both traditional POS and mobile payment processing options. The institution also provides financial calculators and a Knowledge Center for customer education.

As a traditional community bank, URSB serves individuals and small businesses seeking local banking relationships with standard FDIC protections. However, as a regional institution, they may have more limited digital innovation compared to larger national banks or neobanks, and their product offerings, while comprehensive, are typical of mid-sized community banks rather than specialized or distinctive. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile app and online banking platforms
Early direct deposit (up to 2 days early)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Early direct deposit access up to 2 days early with no enrollment required (up to $10,000)
Local discount network (BaZing) providing merchant discounts in Carteret area
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and business lending
24/7 merchant services technical support through TIB partnership
Multiple digital banking channels: mobile app, online banking, and remote deposit
Community engagement through cultural sponsorship and financial literacy initiatives
cons: Limited geographic footprint as regional Carteret, NJ-based institution may restrict branch access for non-local customers
Direct deposit early pay feature capped at $10,000 per deposit (larger deposits post on standard date)
No specific information provided about competitive interest rates on savings or CDs relative to market
Merchant services limited to partnership with single processor (TIB) rather than multiple options
Limited transparency on personal loan terms, rates, and maximum loan amounts on website
best_for: Local Carteret, New Jersey residents seeking community bank relationships with FDIC protection
Small business owners needing merchant services and business banking from a local institution
Employees receiving regular direct deposits who value early access to funds
Customers interested in local discount networks and community-supported banking
diagnosis: URSB is best for local Carteret residents and small business owners seeking traditional FDIC-insured community banking with personalized service and local merchant discounts. The main caveat is limited geographic reach as a regional bank, making it less suitable for customers outside the New Jersey area or those seeking national bank features and branch networks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
